what_it_does: Renders the 'Model Bench' workspace: a dashboard that lists empirical benchmark results for Vi image-generation tasks, shows auto-derived default model routing, displays a recent-run audit trail, and surfaces a qualitative pattern note.
observations: Hardcoded benchmarks array with four tasks and per-model runs including score, latency, cost, QA flags, and a winner flag
Score bars are color-coded by threshold: green above 90, gold 80-90, orange below 80
Winner runs get a gold outline and a 'Winner' chip
Default routing is read from window.MS_DATA.defaultRouting and rendered as job-to-model pairs
Recent runs list shows state name, model, result chip, and relative timestamp
A ViNote at the bottom summarizes a cost/quality/latency tradeoff pattern
Uses a destructured React effect alias: useEffectB
Relies on several undeclared-in-file globals: Btn, Panel, ProviderBadge, Chip, Icon, ViNote, window.ViContext, window.MS_DATA
standards: Combines benchmark leaderboard, routing config, and recent-run history in one workspace
Surfaces a concrete quality/cost/latency tradeoff insight inline
Winner styling and score-bar thresholds make comparisons scannable
conventions: Follows a React functional-component pattern with JSX return
Uses global window.* APIs for context and data
Mixes utility CSS classes with inline style objects
Aliases a destructured hook (useEffectB) to avoid naming collision or for clarity
concerns: All benchmark and recent-run data is hardcoded, not fetched or parameterized
Multiple UI primitives and data globals are referenced but not defined in this file
Icon-only lock button lacks an accessible label
Inline styles are scattered and may complicate theming/maintenance
notes: A benchmark-and-routing dashboard component that visualizes model performance and system routing choices for Vi tasks.
questions: Where does window.MS_DATA.defaultRouting originate and how is it kept in sync with benchmarks?
Are the benchmark scores static fixtures or populated from an API?
What do the QA flags represent and how are they counted?
How is the winner flag determined—automatically or manually pinned?
What is the real provider/model mapping behind names like 'Nano Banana Pro'?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the header and subhead matches the rendered content: it displays empirical model performance and auto-derived routing.
intention_signals: Provide a single workspace where users can compare which model performs best for each Vi task, see how the system currently routes jobs, review recent outcomes, and decide when to promote a cheaper or slower model.
novelty: It frames model evaluation around project-space reference tasks and pairs the leaderboard with both live routing and a recent-run audit trail in one view.
contribution: A workspace UI pattern that displays per-task model benchmark cards with score, latency, cost, and QA flags; exposes default routing mappings; and includes a recent-run history panel and qualitative insight note.
summary_for_embedding: A React workspace component that renders an empirical model benchmark dashboard for Vi image-generation tasks, including per-task score cards, auto-derived default routing, recent run history, and a qualitative pattern note.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to let users compare model performance across Vi tasks and see or override the system's default model routing.
